baseline.notes: Run 50 sporting_ac with field_crop (no upscale): F1 0.210, P 0.515, R 0.132, TP 17/129, FP 16. Coarse firing rate 6.5% vs Rush 30%. Run 51 adds LANCZOS upscale of the cropped image to long-edge=1920 (1280x~540 -> 1920x~810), keeping same field content but giving the VLM more 28x28 patches over it. Tests whether attention capacity on cropped images is the bottleneck.

targets.notes: If upscale is net positive, F1 climbs meaningfully. If ~flat, we've learned the VLM isn't bottlenecked by visual-token count — move to Run 52 (ball-centric crops) which would actually add new information via YOLO-driven framing.

field_crop_upscale_long_edge.reason: Upscale each field crop so its long edge = 1920 via LANCZOS. Visual token budget: 5 * 476 = 2380 tokens + prompt/output ~1400 = 3780, fits 8192 max_model_len with margin. Smoke test on sporting_ac t=428.2: orig 1280x720 -> crop 1280x~540 -> upscaled 1920x816, JPEG 59KB -> 147KB.

notes: Run #51 (commit 7e1c561): field_crop + LANCZOS upscale. Worker restarted at 17:29:01Z, config verified live. Smoke test on one frame confirmed no visible artifacts from interpolation.
